title: Makefile unconditionally excludes device-mapper
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The build instructions at https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#build-and-run-dependencies list `libdevmapper` as a prerequisite, however the `Makefile` unconditionally excludes it:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v2.1.1/Makefile#L31

Is the exclusion intentional?
This seem either to be a build or doc issue.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `./hack/libdm_tag.sh && make 2>&1 | grep -o exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Either
```
libdm_no_deferred_remove
exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper
```
or no output. (Or build instruction change?)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Yes - there were a number of locking issues that rendered devicemapper unusable with Podman's multi-process architecture. I've since been told that this should be fixed, but we've never had any demand to reenable it and test that.

I see, thank you for the explanation.

I was about to suggest that I can open a PR to address the build instructions at https://github.com/containers/podman.io/blob/d3d4e064a64636feaf3c59a6c148e0f154eddeae/getting-started/installation.md#build-and-run-dependencies
-- but then I noticed that I must've overlooked the explicit comment for that before:
> Note that Podman does not officially support device-mapper. Thus, the `exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper` tag is mandatory.

Since this is in fact already mentioned in the docs, let's close this as a "reporter needs to read more carefully" bug ;).
Sorry for the bother and thank you for taking your time to explain the reason behind this.!
